channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump says Russia and Ukraine have agreed to his request for a 3-day ceasefire
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Yuri Ushakov, President Vladimir Putin's foreign affairs adviser, both confirmed the agreement for a three-day ceasefire and an exchange of prisoners.
item.content:encoded:
President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Yuri Ushakov, President Vladimir Putin's foreign affairs adviser, both confirmed the agreement for a three-day ceasefire and an exchange of prisoners.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Organizers of a canceled human rights conference in Zambia say China intervened
item.description: Zambia was due to host Rights Con, the world's biggest digital rights gathering, this week -- but abruptly canceled the event. Organizers blame Chinese pressure for the decision.
item.content:encoded: Zambia was due to host Rights Con, the world's biggest digital rights gathering, this week -- but abruptly canceled the event. Organizers blame Chinese pressure for the decision.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Russia's Victory Day celebrations are dampened by ongoing war with Ukraine
item.description: World War 2 Victory Day celebrations in Moscow will be muted this year because of fears of drone attacks, as the public tires of the Ukraine war.
item.content:encoded: World War 2 Victory Day celebrations in Moscow will be muted this year because of fears of drone attacks, as the public tires of the Ukraine war.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Colombia's rogue hippos could find refuge in India
item.description: In Colombia, a plan to cull Pablo Escobar's invasive hippos is challenged by an Indian billionaire's offer to relocate dozens of the animals to India's wildlife reserve instead.
item.content:encoded:
In Colombia, a plan to cull Pablo Escobar's invasive hippos is challenged by an Indian billionaire's offer to relocate dozens of the animals to India's wildlife reserve instead.

(Image credit: Fernando Vergara)
item.dc:creator: Manuel Rueda

item.title: U.S. intercepts Iranian attacks on 3 ships. And, what to know about hantavirus
item.description: The U.S. says it intercepted Iranian attacks yesterday targeting three Navy ships. And, what to know about the hantavirus outbreak that started on a cruise ship.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. says it intercepted Iranian attacks yesterday targeting three Navy ships. And, what to know about the hantavirus outbreak that started on a cruise ship.

(Image credit: Amirhossein Khorgooei)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The U.S. fires on Iranian tankers trying to evade its blockade amid a Hormuz standoff
item.description: U.S. forces fired on two Iranian oil tankers after exchanging fire with Iranian forces in the Strait of Hormuz. And the United Arab Emirates reported another Iranian missile and drone attack.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. forces fired on two Iranian oil tankers after exchanging fire with Iranian forces in the Strait of Hormuz. And the United Arab Emirates reported another Iranian missile and drone attack.

(Image credit: Amirhossein Khorgooei)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Military drills on the edge: U.S. and allies test capabilities near Asia's flash points
item.description: From drone boats to long-range missiles, the U.S. and its allies tested new military tactics in the Philippines, sharpening deterrence as tensions with China intensify.
item.content:encoded:
From drone boats to long-range missiles, the U.S. and its allies tested new military tactics in the Philippines, sharpening deterrence as tensions with China intensify.

(Image credit: Anthony Kuhn)
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: Spain readies for evacuations as a hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for Canary Islands
item.description: Spanish authorities are preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands.
item.content:encoded:
Spanish authorities are preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands.

(Image credit: Misper Apawu)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: David Attenborough celebrates his 100th birthday
item.description: He was born before the Great Depression, came of age in WWII, and is still making wildlife documentaries. Brits call David Attenborough a national hero, as he celebrates his 100th birthday.
item.content:encoded: He was born before the Great Depression, came of age in WWII, and is still making wildlife documentaries. Brits call David Attenborough a national hero, as he celebrates his 100th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Fallout from the Iran war may include a NATO where the U.S. is no longer its leader
item.description: President Trump's decision to leave NATO in the dark before launching strikes on Iran has inflamed tensions and is putting new urgency on rethinking the alliance.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's decision to leave NATO in the dark before launching strikes on Iran has inflamed tensions and is putting new urgency on rethinking the alliance.

(Image credit: Joern Pollex)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman
